scope: Structural engineering across all material types (steel, concrete, timber, composites, masonry). Infrastructure engineering, earthquake engineering, structural dynamics, structure-fluid-soil interaction, wind engineering, fire engineering, blast engineering, structural reliability/stability, structural health monitoring, multi-hazard engineering, structural optimization, digital design methods, data-driven analysis, experimental methods, performance-based design, additive manufacturing, tall buildings, bridges, stadiums, smart structures.

manuscript_structure.abstract_note: Concise and factual. Must not exceed 250 words. State purpose, principal results, major conclusions. Avoid references.
manuscript_structure.keywords_note: Avoid keywords with multiple words using and/of.
manuscript_structure.highlights_note: 3-5 bullet points, max 85 characters each including spaces
manuscript_structure.novelty_note: Manuscripts must clearly state novel contributions compared to existing literature
manuscript_structure.case_study_note: Case studies and multi-part papers generally NOT accepted unless clear scientific innovation
manuscript_structure.review_article_note: Review articles require prior proposal to Editors-in-Chief

formatting.your_paper_your_way: Flexible formatting for initial submission; strict formatting at revision stage

references.format: Numbered in square brackets [1] in order of appearance
examples: [1] S. Kim, J. Lee, Convolutional neural network for seismic damage classification of RC structures, Eng. Struct. 289 (2024) 116234.
[2] A.K. Chopra, Dynamics of Structures: Theory and Applications to Earthquake Engineering, fifth ed., Pearson, Hoboken, NJ, 2020.
[3] EN 1998-1, Eurocode 8: Design of Structures for Earthquake Resistance – Part 1, European Committee for Standardization, Brussels, 2004.
references.formatting_rules: Author surname then initials (no periods between initials). Article titles in regular text. Journal names in italics. Format: Journal Volume (Year) Pages. DOI highly encouraged.

figures_tables.color_note: Free online; check for print color. Use RGB encoding.

submission_notes.units: SI units required. If other units used, SI equivalent must be provided.
submission_notes.equations: Displayed separately, numbered consecutively: Eq. (1), Eq. (2). In appendices: Eq. (A.1).
submission_notes.math_notation: Italic for scalar variables, bold for vectors/matrices
submission_notes.ethical_requirements: Original unpublished work. All authors must use institutional/professional email.
submission_notes.author_list_note: Author list frozen once submitted for initial review
submission_notes.ai_policy: AI tools may support preparation but never substitute for human expertise. Authors fully responsible.
submission_notes.referee_suggestions: Authors must name independent referees with institutional emails, not from own institution
